notes: SHIP v1.14.290 - Ship 4 CHUNK 2 (R5 red/green removal from the blocker modal). blocker_render + sub-renderers carried off-palette iOS RED (#ff453a x4 + rgba(255,69,58,x) x10 = 14 red instances) and off-palette GREEN (rgba(48,209,88,x) x3, the NEW FROM MASTER CTA). R5: fault = MAGENTA only; no green in the house. Recolored via a Node splice scoped to function blocker_render()...function blocker_close() (so the unrelated #ff453a at deploy_showTools ~L26787 and 17 other off-scope reds are untouched): red -> mag (#ff453a->#ff2bd6, rgba(255,69,58,x)->rgba(255,43,214,x)); green CTA -> cyan (rgba(48,209,88,x)->rgba(40,224,255,x); a primary create action = cyan/active). SKIN ONLY - recon confirmed every token is a literal in a render-function template string; DOM ids + the blocker_save->deploy_advancePhase write path are colour-independent and unchanged. CRLF preserved (utf8 splice); blocker segment now 0 red / 0 green. Combined with .289's .pbar-glow deletion, the Command house is clear of off-palette red+green EXCEPT out-of-fence: the SYS-critical header glow (#ff3b3b L9253, shared-header legacy trap - separate ruling) and .stat.block red TEXT (L9149). Three-stamp .289->.290. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone): open a blocker (hero BLOCKER verb) -> modal reads MAGENTA (fault), not iOS-red; the empty-state NEW FROM MASTER button is cyan not green; capture still writes the blocker (same behavior). NOTE: green-CTA->cyan is Claude's channel inference; flag if you want a different channel. Remaining Ship-4: .spill OK-dot green, .tile idle-bloom->dark, and the design-heavy parts (askrow/reserved-guard/hero-mag-strip/BLOCKER-verb-accent/one-global-state->channel-helper).
